avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: New unstable windslab will develop mainly on North through East to South-East aspects above 900 metres. Greatest hazard will be found on steep wind sheltered locations such as coire rims and gully tops where accumulations will develop over older snow-ice and only be moderately bonded. Old snow will be very firm and icy.  Cornices will start to redevelop. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Snowfall is expected, down to the glens overnight but generally above 600 metres during the day. Gale force winds overnight but easing slightly during the day, initially South-West, veering during the day to the West.
Observed Weather Influences: Precipitation falling as snow around the higher summits by mid morning, otherwise, rain at all other levels. Freezing level had dropped to 900 metres by afternoon. Winds were storm force, but easing to gale force in the afternoon.
ObservedSnowStability: The majority of the snowpack is thawing at all levels but remains generally well bonded. New shallow snow accumulations were limited to around corrie rims and gully tops mainly on North to East aspects above 950 metres. Cornice stability remains suspect.  The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Fresh unstable windslab will develop through the period mainly on North-West through North to South-East aspects above 800 metres. Fragile cornices will develop above these aspects. The older snowpack will become firm and stable in the lower temperatures. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Gale force South-Westerly winds overnight will ease to become strong during the day. Snow showers throughout the period, heaviest overnight. The freezing level will be around 400 metres overnight rising to 600 metres during the day.
Observed Weather Influences: Showers fell as snow to gradually lower levels through the day. Storm force West-South-Westerly winds eased to become gale force. The freezing level dropped from 1250 metres in the morning to 600 metres in the afternoon.
ObservedSnowStability: Areas of fresh unstable windslab are present mainly on North-West through North to South-East aspects above 1000 metres. Fragile cornices are present above these aspects. The older snowpack has good stability. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Unstable windslab will continue to develop overnight and during the day becoming more widespread and deeper on Northerly to Easterly aspects above 850 metres. Coire rims, headwalls and gullies especially affected. Cornices will also build above same aspects with collapse possible. Below 850m and elsewhere new snow will mostly lie on bare ground and be stable. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Winds gale force South-Westerly overnight veering West-South-West during the day but remaining strong. Persistent snow fall throughout the forecast period above 200m overnight then above 500m or so as the freezing level rises slightly.
Observed Weather Influences: Heavy precipitation overnight and during the morning mostly falling as rain then sleet and snow at summit levels. Gradually turning to snow with heavy showers late morning, then down to 600m or so early afternoon as the freezing level dropped. Winds gale force South-Westerly.
ObservedSnowStability: There is a dusting of new snow on most aspects above 600m. Above 900m on Northerly to Easterly aspects new shallow accumulations of snow were mostly moist and stable at the time of observation. However windslab continues to develop becoming deeper and more widespread especially in wind sheltered locations with coire rims and gully tops most affected. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: New snow and wind transport of existing snow will form unstable windslab in wind sheltered areas, most affected will be sheltered gullies and slopes with a North-West through North to Easterly aspect mainly above 800 metres.  Fragile cornices may form in similar locations. The existing snowpack will be generally stable with a firm, icy surface developing in places.  The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: There will be a return to colder conditions with a freezing level through the day of around 600 metres. New snow  and fresh to strong variable South-Westerly winds will affect the area overnight and through the course of Friday.
Observed Weather Influences: Milder conditions overnight gave a period of rain with sleet on the higher slopes. Winds have been strong to gale force generally South-Westerly. The freezing level initially this morning was above the summits but had lowered to around 900 metres by mid day. Light snow showers are expected through Thursday afternoon.
ObservedSnowStability: Surface snow instabilities exist on the steepest slopes and gullies mainly above 1000 metres, these are in the main avoidable, elsewhere the snowpack is soft moist and generally stable.  The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Accumulations of poorly bonded windslab will become more widespread on North to East aspects above 950 metres. These will be more localised on North-West and South-East aspects, with steep banks and burn lines below 950 metres also affected. On these aspects, fragile cornices will develop. Elsewhere the snowpack will be firm wind scoured and stable. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Snow showers are expected throughout the forecast period. Gale force South-Westerly winds will ease to strong during the day and veer to become West-South-Westerly.  The freezing level will rise from 400 metres to around 700 metres during the day.
Observed Weather Influences: Heavy rain with snowfall above 1000 metres overnight; this became more showery during the day, turning to snow at lower levels by mid afternoon. Winds were gale to storm force South-Westerly in direction. The freezing level fell to 1000 metres by midday.
ObservedSnowStability: Accumulations of moist moderately bonded windslab have developed on North to East aspects above 900 metres. The greatest instability is in steep wind sheltered terrain such as the tops of gullies, ridge flanks and coire rims. Wet snow instabilities were observed on a steep North aspect at 1050 metres. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: A layer of fresh generally stable snow will develop above around 500 metres mostly lying on bare ground. Localised areas of generally shallow windslab will develop gradually through the period only beginning to gain depth towards the end of the day.  Wind sheltered areas such as gullies and coire rims with a North to East aspect above 800 metres will be most affected. The existing patchy snow cover will remain well bonded and stable. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Showers throughout the forecast period will fall as snow above around 500m and be heaviest overnight. Very strong South-Westerly winds overnight easing during the day.
Observed Weather Influences: Heavy rain on very strong South-Westerly winds, first thing this morning, turned to snow above around 700 metres then eased by mid morning. The rest of the day was mostly dry with occasional showers and strong South-Westerly winds. The freezing level was around 700 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: The existing patchy snow cover is well bonded and stable. A superficial layer of new snow lies on many aspects above around 700 metres. The avalanche hazard is Low.
